hawkish_phrases: One member preferred to raise the target range for the federal funds rate at the current meeting
some moderation in employment gains was to be expected
risk to the economic expansion that would be associated with allowing labor market conditions to tighten to an extent that could lead to an unwanted buildup of inflation pressures
concern that an extended period of low interest rates risked intensifying incentives for investors to reach for yield
labor market conditions were at or close to those consistent with maximum employment
dovish_phrases: before taking another step in removing monetary accommodation, it was prudent to accumulate more data
wait for more evidence that inflation would rise to 2 percent on a sustained basis
Inflation has continued to run below the Committee's 2 percent longer-run objective
In light of the current shortfall of inflation from 2 percent, the Committee will carefully monitor actual and expected progress toward its inflation goal
Near-term risks to the economic outlook have diminished
summary: The Committee voted 9-1 to hold the target range at 1/4 to 1/2 percent with Esther George dissenting in favor of a 25bp hike, maintaining an accommodative stance with clear emphasis on waiting for more data before removing accommodation despite reduced near-term risks.
